svg.text: Plutonium-pit carrying box — replica assembly (front cutaway)
7.25 in (184 mm) cube · Richlite phenolic body · split 1/3 from bottom · sphere + Urchin in central cavity

g.text: Delrin top & bottom scales, brass alignment pins
3 — Urchin port plug — turned aluminum cap,
(×4 perimeter, into Helicoil inserts below split)
5 — Cooling fins — 3 mm side-mill, 2 mm deep,
front face, spans the split line)
9 — Body split line — 1/3 up from bottom face
retains sphere when box is closed
0.25 mm kerf, A/B punch marks on rim
(Urchin home; ball-mill 12.5 mm deep each half)
17 — Urchin initiator replica, 20 mm OD brass,
gold-painted, 15 wedge grooves on inner face
18 — 8 mm inner Be pellet (brass in replica),
cross-pinned to outer shell with 1.5 mm brass pin
at 12/6 o'clock on cavity rim — lifts sphere out
21 — Finish — Box 1: PJ1 Fast Black Wrinkle, baked
MT-17F WNF tungsten heavy alloy, 17 g/cm³ (sphere)
2. Face-mill cube to 7.25″ on all sides
3. Lathe-cut sphere cavity, both halves (radius cutter)
4. Mill ports, thermometer boss, cooling fins, bolt holes
5. Install Helicoils, glue Al port-ring inserts, face-mill flush
6. Drill stoppers; screw bumpers; finish (paint + bake)
